histogram.name: Cras.A2dp100msFailureOverStream
histogram.summary: For each of bluetooth A2DP stream period, track the sum of all period lengths of consecutive packet write failures observerd by CRAS that is longer than 100ms.

histogram.name: Cras.A2dp20msFailureOverStream
histogram.summary: For each of bluetooth A2DP stream period, track the sum of all period lengths of consecutive packet write failures observerd by CRAS that is longer than 20ms.

histogram.name: Cras.A2dpExitCode
histogram.summary: Track all different paths causing bluetooth A2DP drop from audio output settings. Collect this metrics to help investigate possible issues and monitor the trend to catch regressions.

histogram.name: Cras.ApNcRuntime
histogram.summary: Tracks the runtime of the APM when AP NC is active.

histogram.name: Cras.ApNcStartStatus
histogram.summary: Sent when AP NC is requested in APM. 0 for AP NC requested but failed; 1 for AP NC requested and success.

histogram.name: Cras.AstRuntime
histogram.summary: Tracks the runtime of the APM when AST is active.

histogram.name: Cras.AstStartStatus
histogram.summary: Sent when AST is requested in APM. 0 for AST requested but failed; 1 for AST requested and success.

histogram.name: Cras.Busyloop
histogram.summary: For each audio thread running state, records the number of busyloops. Busyloop means the audio thread doesn't sleep during more than two loops, which may make the system busy. Depending on the time of running state, the records is separated into three logs: ShortPeriod(shorter than 10 minutes), MediumPeriod(between 10 and 60 minutes), LongPeriod(longer than 60 minutes).

histogram.name: Cras.BusyloopLength
histogram.summary: When each busyloop stops, records the length of it. The lengths exceeding 1000 are recorded as 1000.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceConfigureTime.Input{Device}{HostCapability}{PeripheralCapability}
histogram.summary: The configure time of input device {Device} in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The configure time is the time taken for the device's own configure function to execute and when it returns signaling that device is ready to send/receive audio data. For wired audio devices this is expected to range from 10ms to 100ms, while wireless headsets this could take longer than 500ms. We collect this configure time to gather stats and track the improvement on shortening this configure time.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceConfigureTime.Output{Device}{HostCapability}{PeripheralCapability}
histogram.summary: The configure time of output device {Device} in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The configure time is the time taken for the device's own configure function to execute and when it returns signaling that device is ready to send/receive audio data. For wired audio devices this is expected to range from 10ms to 100ms, while wireless headsets this could take longer than 500ms. We collect this configure time to gather stats and track the improvement on shortening this configure time.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceDspOffloadStatus{Device}
histogram.summary: Record the staus of DSP offload for the supported device. It is recorded when the configuration of associated DSP modules is changed. That is, it can be regarded as the DSP configuration error rate with attempt to DSP offload.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceGain{Device}
histogram.summary: The gain of a device which is recorded when closing a device. The original value of UI gain scaler is between 0 to 10. The gain here has been multiplied by 100.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceNoiseCancellationStatus{Device}
histogram.summary: Record the staus of Noise Cancellation (blocked/disabled/enabled) for the supported device. It is recorded when the device is opened.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceOpenStatus.Input{Device}
histogram.summary: Log the result every time CRAS tries to open an input device.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceOpenStatus.Output{Device}
histogram.summary: Log the result every time CRAS tries to open an output device.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceRuntime.Input{Device}{HostCapability}{PeripheralCapability}
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device {Device} in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed. For certain devices, more detailed host/peripheral-capabilities are recorded. The aggregated runtime for each level is accessible up to "Cras.DeviceRuntime".

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceRuntime.Output{Device}{HostCapability}{PeripheralCapability}
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device {Device} in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed. For certain devices, more detailed host/peripheral-capabilities are recorded. The aggregated runtime for each level is accessible up to "Cras.DeviceRuntime".

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceSampleRate.Input{Device}
histogram.summary: The sampling rate of a device. It is recorded when the input device is opened.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceSampleRate.Output{Device}
histogram.summary: The sampling rate of a device. It is recorded when the output device is opened.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceSamplesDropped{Device}
histogram.summary: The number of samples dropped from a device. It is recorded when an input device is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceTypeInput
histogram.summary: It is used for logging input device usage of ChromeOS users. The count indicates the number of each input device has been used and is recorded when a device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceTypeOutput
histogram.summary: It is used for logging output device usage of ChromeOS users. The count indicates the number of each input device has been used and is recorded when a device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceVolume{Device}
histogram.summary: The volume of a device, which is recorded when closing a device.

histogram.name: Cras.DlcManagerStatus.DlcStatus.{DlcType}
histogram.summary: Records if {DlcType} is successfully installed or not (failed all retries).

histogram.name: Cras.DlcManagerStatus.ElapsedTimeHistogramOnFailure.{DlcType}
histogram.summary: Records the elapsed time of installing {DlcType} when failure occurs.

histogram.name: Cras.DlcManagerStatus.ElapsedTimeHistogramOnSuccess.{DlcType}
histogram.summary: If the installation of {DlcType} succeeded, records the elapsed time.

histogram.name: Cras.FetchDelayCount
histogram.summary: The number of times that CRAS(ChromeOS audio server) ever waits for a stream exceeding the timeout threshold. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on ChromeOS.

histogram.name: Cras.FetchDelayMilliSeconds
histogram.summary: The longest additional time CRAS(ChromeOS audio server) ever waits for a stream exceeding the timeout threshold. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on ChromeOS.

histogram.name: Cras.ForceRespectUiGains
histogram.summary: Records the user enables or not the force respect UI gains. This value is recorded for each change to the force respect ui gains setting.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpBatteryIndicatorSupported
histogram.summary: For each HFP audio device record whether it supports battery indicator.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpBatteryReport
histogram.summary: For each HFP battery level change event record if it comes from the command defined by Apple's Spec or BT HFP spec. The count indicates how frequent user might see this event and can help us decide which data source we prefer to show if the headset supports both.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpMicSuperResolutionStatus.{Device}
histogram.summary: Record the status of Super Resolution for the bluetooth microphone audio. It is recorded when the device is opened. Expired Sept 2023 and revived Jan 2024.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpScoConnectionError
histogram.summary: For each SCO connection record the returned event. The event indicates if the setup of the connection is successful or not.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpTelephonyEvent
histogram.summary: Record the Bluetooth hfp telephony event when CRAS receives the event from Floss. This is for tracking how users interact with the Bluetooth telephony feature.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpWidebandSpeechPacketLoss
histogram.summary: For each HFP audio device supporting WBS record how many packets are lost per 1000 packets when the device is closed. This is for tracking how bad the packet loss can be for WBS in real world.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpWidebandSpeechSupported
histogram.summary: For each single connection of bluetooth HFP audio device record whether it supports wideband-speech feature or not.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestDeviceDelayInput
histogram.summary: The highest hardware delay of input device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). It is calculated by 1000 * highest_hw_level / largest_cb_level. The highest_hw_level is the highest remaining frames in the device. The largest_cb_level is the largest callback threshold of streams which have attached to this device. The value is recorded when the device is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestDeviceDelayOutput
histogram.summary: The highest hardware delay of output device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). It is calculated by 1000 * highest_hw_level / largest_cb_level. The highest_hw_level is the highest remaining frames in the device. The largest_cb_level is the largest callback threshold of streams which have attached to this device. The value is recorded when the device is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestInputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of input device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For input device, it can show the latency between capturing and actual reading.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestOutputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of output device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For output device, it can show the latency between writing and actual playout.

histogram.name: Cras.InputGainChanged
histogram.summary: Records the source of a change to the input gain if users don't force to respect UI gains.

histogram.name: Cras.InputGainChangedSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of a change to the input gain.

histogram.name: Cras.InputGainMutedSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of muting/unmuting the input gain.

histogram.name: Cras.InternalSoundcardStatus{Second}
histogram.summary: Checks whether an internal soundcard has been detected after CRAS runs for {Second} seconds.

histogram.name: Cras.kHfpWidebandSpeechSelectedCodec
histogram.summary: For each single connection of bluetooth HFP record the result of codec negotiation, if supported.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFirstTimeInput
histogram.summary: The first time missed callbacks happened on input streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). In normal situation, audio thread should follow the callback schedule to send frames to clients. However, sometimes it may miss schedule because of some reasons. When the first time that audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since stream started running.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFirstTimeOutput
histogram.summary: The first time missed callbacks happened on output streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). In normal situation, audio thread should follow the callback schedule to fetch frames from clients. However, sometimes it may miss schedule because of some reasons. When the first time that audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since stream started running.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyAfterReschedulingInput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of input streams in a day after rescheduling. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream after rescheduling. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyAfterReschedulingOutput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of output streams in a day after rescheduling. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream after rescheduling. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyInput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of input streams in a day. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyOutput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of output streams in a day. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackSecondTimeInput
histogram.summary: The second time missed callbacks happened on input streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). When the second time audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since the first time.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackSecondTimeOutput
histogram.summary: The second time missed callbacks happened on output streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). When the second time audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since the first time.

histogram.name: Cras.NoiseCancellationEnabledSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of enabling/disabling noise cancellation.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputVolumeChangedSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of a change to the output volume.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputVolumeMutedSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of muting/unmuting the output volume.

histogram.name: Cras.PeerSupportedA2dpCodecs
histogram.summary:
Records the supported A2DP codecs claimed by the connected BT headset.
The emitted value is a bitmask whose bits have the following meaning:
Bit #1 is true iff peer supports SBC.
Bit #2 is true iff peer supports AAC.

histogram.name: Cras.RtcDevicePair
histogram.summary: After one webRTC stream is completed, the corresponding device pair will be logged. The runtime shorter than 1s will be ignored.

histogram.name: Cras.RtcRuntime.{InDevice}.{OutDevice}
histogram.summary: The runtime of webRTC streams using input device {InDevice} and output device {OutDevice}. This log will be logged after a stream is completed or devices are changed.

histogram.name: Cras.SetAecRefDeviceType
histogram.summary: It is used to log the call to InputStream::SetOutputDeviceForAec() which results different types of audio device being set as AEC referefence in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). Based on this metrics we can analyze the user behavior in voice communication app. Note that CRAS_METRICS_DEVICE_NO_DEVICE is tracking the case when the call sets a NULL device meaning to leave AEC reference on system default.

histogram.name: Cras.SoundCardInit.CalibrationResult
histogram.summary: Logs the smart amp calibration result at each boot time.

histogram.name: Cras.SoundCardInit.ExitCode
histogram.summary: Logs the sound_card_init exit code at each boot time.

histogram.name: Cras.SoundCardInit.WaitForSpeaker
histogram.summary: Logs the whether the speakers are ready for SoundCardInit at boot time.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamAddError
histogram.summary: Record the errors happen when CRAS server adss a stream to its audio thread.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamCallbackThreshold
histogram.summary: The callback threshold of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the number of frames when to request more from the client. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamChannelCount
histogram.summary: The channel count of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamClientTypeInput
histogram.summary: The client type of input stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamClientTypeOutput
histogram.summary: The client type of output stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamConnectStatus
histogram.summary: Record the status when CRAS server handles the client requests to connect new streams.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamCreateError
histogram.summary: Record the errors happen when CRAS server creats a stream.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamEffects
histogram.summary: The bit map of effects to be enabled on this stream. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamEffectState{Effect}
histogram.summary: The state of system {Effect} set for a stream. This value is recorded when the stream is started.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamFlags
histogram.summary: The flags of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the special handling for stream. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamOverrunCount
histogram.summary: The number of overruns happened on a stream. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on ChromeOS.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamOverrunFrames
histogram.summary: The number of overrun frames on a stream. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on ChromeOS.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of stream in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between creating and destroying a stream. The value is recorded when the stream is destroyed.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamRuntimeWithMinimum{Duration}{Direction}{ClientType}{StreamType}
histogram.summary: Similar to Cras.StreamRuntime, but only for streams with runtime at least {Duration}. The value is recorded when the stream is destroyed.

variant.name: .CrOSVM
variant.summary: A client created by ChromeOS VM.

variant.name: .Legacy
variant.summary: A client using an old CRAS lib.

variant.name: .ServerStream
variant.summary: A client created by CRAS server.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingFormat
histogram.summary: The sampling format of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingRate
histogram.summary: The sampling rate of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.UnderrunsPerDeviceDuringAPNC{Device}
histogram.summary: The number of underruns happening when AP NC is running. The metric will not be sent if there is no NC enabled during the device runtime.

histogram.name: Cras.UnderrunsPerDevice{Device}
histogram.summary: The number of underruns happened on one audio device. Underrun here means there are no samples in device buffer while device is playing. It is recorded when device is closed by CRAS(Chrome OS audio server).

histogram.name: Cras.WakeDelay
histogram.summary: Logs how long the audio thread delays to wake up. It's logged every time the audio thread delays to wake up. Delays less than 1ms are ignored.

histogram.name: Cras.WakeDelayCountPer10kWakes
histogram.summary: Logs how many times the audio thread delayed waking up per 10k wakes. Delays less than 1ms are ignored.

histogram.name: Cras.WebRTC.Audio.ApmCaptureInputLevelAverageRms
histogram.summary: This histogram reports the peak RMS of the signal in the output of WebRTC's Audio Processing Module run in CRAS, after all WebRTC audio processing. A new value is reported every 10 seconds, and the peak is the RMS of the strongest 10 ms block over the latest interval. The metric is negated dBFS, meaning that 0 is a full-scale signal, while 127 corresponds to -127 dBFS (very faint).

histogram.name: Cras.WebRTC.Audio.ApmCaptureInputLevelPeakRms
histogram.summary: This histogram reports the peak RMS of the signal coming in to WebRTC's Audio Processing Module run in CRAS, prior to any WebRTC processing. A new value is reported every 10 seconds, and the peak is the RMS of the strongest 10 ms block over the latest interval. The metric is negated dBFS, meaning that 0 is a full-scale signal, while 127 corresponds to -127 dBFS (very faint).

histogram.name: Cras.WebRTC.Audio.ApmCaptureOutputLevelAverageRms
histogram.summary: This histogram reports the average RMS of the signal in the output of WebRTC's Audio Processing Module run in CRAS, after all audio WebRTC processing. A new value is reported every 10 seconds, and the average is over the latest interval. The metric is negated dBFS, meaning that 0 is a full-scale signal, while 127 corresponds to -127 dBFS (very faint).

histogram.name: Cras.WebRTC.Audio.ApmCaptureOutputLevelPeakRms
histogram.summary: This histogram reports the peak RMS of the signal in the output of WebRTC's Audio Processing Module run in CRAS, after all WebRTC audio processing. A new value is reported every 10 seconds, and the peak is the RMS of the strongest 10 ms block over the latest interval. The metric is negated dBFS, meaning that 0 is a full-scale signal, while 127 corresponds to -127 dBFS (very faint).
